prerequisites: An AWS account with an Amazon S3 bucket in the region you intend to write to. Provide the bucket name in s3_bucket_name, the prefix in s3_bucket_path, and the region in s3_bucket_region.

An AWS Glue Data Catalog database that already exists in the same region. Provide its name in glue_database; this connector registers and updates table metadata in that database so downstream services can query the files written to S3.

An output format selected in format (the underlying Airbyte S3 destination supports Avro, CSV, JSONL, and Parquet) along with the Glue serialization library setting in glue_serialization_library that matches the data files written to S3.

AWS credentials with permission to write to the S3 bucket and to manage tables in the Glue database. Supply an IAM user access key (access_key_id and secret_access_key), or, when running on AWS compute, omit them to use the host instance profile or role.

S3 object permissions for the IAM identity: s3:PutObject, s3:GetObject, s3:DeleteObject, s3:PutObjectAcl, s3:ListBucket, s3:ListBucketMultipartUploads, s3:AbortMultipartUpload, and s3:GetBucketLocation on the target bucket and its objects.

AWS Glue Data Catalog permissions for the IAM identity to create and update tables in the database, for example glue:GetDatabase, glue:GetTable, glue:CreateTable, and glue:UpdateTable scoped to the catalog, the database, and its tables.

authMethods.name: IAM user access key (access_key_id / secret_access_key)
authMethods.summary: Authenticate to AWS with a long-lived IAM user access key. Set access_key_id to the Access Key ID and secret_access_key to the Secret Access Key. The IAM user must hold the S3 object permissions and the Glue Data Catalog table permissions described in the prerequisites.

authMethods.name: Instance profile / role-based credentials (omit access key)
authMethods.summary: When the connector runs on AWS compute, leave access_key_id and secret_access_key empty (both are optional) and the connector uses the host's attached IAM instance profile or role. That role must carry the same S3 and Glue permissions as an IAM user would.

setupSteps.title: Create the S3 bucket
setupSteps.body: In the AWS console, create or choose an S3 bucket in the region you will sync to. Record the bucket name for s3_bucket_name and the region for s3_bucket_region. Choose a prefix inside the bucket for s3_bucket_path where the data files will be written.

setupSteps.title: Create the Glue Data Catalog database
setupSteps.body: In AWS Glue, create the Data Catalog database that will hold the table metadata for the synced streams, in the same region as the bucket. Record its name for glue_database. The connector creates and updates a table per stream in this database pointing at the files in S3.

setupSteps.title: Create an IAM identity and attach permissions
setupSteps.body: Create or select an IAM user or role and attach an IAM policy granting the S3 object actions (s3:PutObject, s3:GetObject, s3:DeleteObject, s3:PutObjectAcl, s3:ListBucket, s3:ListBucketMultipartUploads, s3:AbortMultipartUpload, s3:GetBucketLocation) on the bucket, plus the Glue actions to read and write tables in the catalog and database (for example glue:GetDatabase, glue:GetTable, glue:CreateTable, glue:UpdateTable).

setupSteps.title: Provide credentials
setupSteps.body: For an IAM user, generate an access key and set access_key_id and secret_access_key. When running on AWS compute that has an instance profile or assumed role with the required permissions, leave both fields empty to use those credentials instead.

setupSteps.title: Configure output format and file layout
setupSteps.body: Set format to the desired output format and set glue_serialization_library to the serialization library matching that format so the Glue table can be read. Optionally set s3_path_format to control the object key layout, file_name_pattern to control file names, and s3_endpoint to target an S3-compatible endpoint such as MinIO; leave s3_endpoint empty for standard AWS S3.

sources.title: Identity-based policy examples for AWS Glue (AWS Glue Developer Guide)
Getting started with the AWS Glue Data Catalog
